name: Arch Linux
description: Arch Linux is a lightweight, independently-developed rolling-release distribution that follows a keep-it-simple philosophy and ships only upstream-supported software.

name: Manjaro Linux
description: Manjaro is a user-friendly Arch-derived distribution that provides a rolling-release model with curated snapshots and a focus on accessibility for newcomers.

name: Manjaro ARM
description: Manjaro ARM extends the Manjaro experience to ARM-based single-board computers and mobile devices, providing rolling-release builds optimised for the platform.

name: EndeavourOS
description: EndeavourOS is a community-driven Arch-derivative that provides a friendly installer and a near-vanilla Arch experience without the heavy customisation of other forks.

name: Garuda Linux
description: Garuda Linux is a performance- and gaming-oriented Arch derivative that ships with extensive theming, Btrfs snapshots and a curated selection of desktop environments.

name: CachyOS
description: CachyOS is an Arch-derived distribution focused on performance, shipping custom-optimised kernels, Btrfs tuning and a curated desktop experience out of the box.

name: Artix Linux
description: Artix Linux is an Arch-derivative that replaces systemd with alternative init systems (OpenRC, runit or s6) for users who prefer a simpler service manager.

name: BlendOS
description: BlendOS is a hybrid immutable distribution that blends Arch and Debian package sources, offering a containerised system approach with multiple desktop flavours.

name: SteamOS
description: SteamOS is Valve's gaming-focused, immutable Linux distribution that powers the Steam Deck console and is built on top of an Arch base.

name: ChimeraOS
description: ChimeraOS (formerly GamerOS) is a gaming-focused distribution built on Manjaro that turns a PC into a living-room game console with a Steam Big Picture experience.

name: Hyperbola GNU/Linux-libre
description: Hyperbola is a FSF-endorsed Arch fork that strips all non-free components, replaces systemd with OpenRC and aims for long-term stability on top of a curated snapshot base.

name: Parabola GNU/Linux-libre
description: Parabola is a FSF-endorsed Arch-derivative that removes all non-free packages and the Linux-libre kernel, providing a fully free software distribution.

name: InstantOS
description: InstantOS is a lightweight Arch-derivative that ships a custom tiling window manager and configuration layer aimed at fast, keyboard-driven workflows.

name: BigLinux
description: BigLinux is a Brazilian Manjaro-based distribution with a strong focus on usability, multimedia tools and a heavily customised KDE Plasma desktop.

name: Mabox Linux
description: Mabox Linux is a Manjaro-based distribution featuring a custom Openbox configuration geared towards tiling-style workflows and a lightweight desktop feel.

name: AxOS
description: AxOS is a beginner-friendly Arch-based distribution offering a graphical installer and pre-configured desktop environments for an easy entry into the Arch ecosystem.

name: RebornOS
description: RebornOS is an Arch-based distribution that lets users pick from many desktop environments at install time, acting as a versatile gateway into Arch.

name: Carbs Linux
description: Carbs Linux is a minimalist source-based distribution inspired by Arch that compiles packages from source with a custom package manager and tiny init system.

name: Archcraft
description: Archcraft is a lightweight Arch-based distribution focused on tiling and stacking window managers with curated themes for users who prefer minimal, keyboard-driven desktops.

name: ArcoLinux
description: ArcoLinux is an educational Arch-based project that provides curated learning scripts to help users build custom Arch systems with various window managers.

name: ArchLabs
description: ArchLabs is a lightweight Arch-based distribution that pairs a minimal base with a curated Openbox environment for users seeking a low-resource desktop.

name: ArchBang Linux
description: ArchBang is a lightweight Arch-derivative that ships a pre-configured Openbox desktop for users who want an Arch base with minimal setup work.

name: Bluestar Linux
description: Bluestar Linux is a user-friendly Arch-based distribution that bundles codecs, drivers and desktop flavours to make Arch accessible to newcomers.

name: Crystal Linux
description: Crystal Linux is an Arch-derivative that ships an easy installer and a curated set of tools, with the goal of making the Arch experience smoother for everyday users.

name: Arch Linux 32
description: Arch Linux 32 was a community port of Arch Linux to i686 32-bit processors, providing the same rolling-release model for older hardware.

name: Chakra Linux
description: Chakra Linux was an Arch-derivative focused exclusively on the KDE Plasma desktop with a curated half-rolling release model around the KDE stack.

name: Antergos
description: Antergos was a beginner-friendly Arch-based distribution with a graphical installer and several pre-configured desktop environments before its discontinuation in 2019.

name: Bridge Linux
description: Bridge Linux was a short-lived Arch-based distribution that aimed to simplify the Arch installation process with multiple desktop flavours.

name: Obarun
description: Obarun is an Arch-derivative that offers a fully s6-based init system with a philosophy of component composition for users who reject both systemd and OpenRC.

name: MagpieOS
description: MagpieOS is a fresh, performance-oriented Arch-based distribution that emphasises a clean KDE Plasma experience and curated package selection.

name: MakuluLinux
description: MakuluLinux is a desktop-focused distribution whose current editions are built on an Arch base, providing a polished, multi-DE experience with custom theming.

name: SnowflakeOS
description: SnowflakeOS is an Arch-based distribution with a custom installer that offers a refined, bloat-free KDE Plasma experience for desktop users.

name: StormOS
description: StormOS is a community Arch-based distribution that focuses on minimalism and simplicity while providing a custom installer for a quick setup.

name: JaOS
description: JaOS is a small Arch-based distribution offering a minimal, Openbox-driven desktop for users who want a lightweight system with an Arch core.

name: ArchEX
description: ArchEX was a live, Arch-based rescue and demo distribution by exton, providing a pre-configured Arch environment usable from a USB stick.

name: Pebang
description: Pebang was a small Arch-based distribution shipping a pre-configured Openbox environment aimed at lightweight, fast desktop use.

name: Arch Linux ARM
description: Arch Linux ARM is an official port of Arch Linux to ARM-based devices, providing the same rolling-release model and package set for the ARM architecture.

name: BlackArch
description: BlackArch is a security-focused Arch-derivative that adds a repository of more than 2,500 penetration-testing and forensics tools on top of a minimal Arch base.

name: Peropesis
description: Peropesis is a small Arch-derivative aimed at being a minimal, easy-to-use system with a curated selection of tools and a low maintenance footprint.

name: Sulinux
description: Sulinux is an Arch-derivative focused on providing a polished GNOME or KDE experience on top of a clean Arch base for everyday desktop users.

name: SwagArch
description: SwagArch was an Arch-based distribution that emphasised a fashionable, themed user experience alongside the rolling-release Arch model.

name: YiffOS
description: YiffOS is a small community-driven Arch-based distribution that packages a tailored set of software for a friendly rolling-release desktop.
